untranslatable_reason: Signal requires external event detection (related company announcement, sentiment shifts, sector-wide news) and cross-asset correlation analysis. These are fundamentally macro/news-driven inputs not available in the price/technical indicator universe. The 'rub-off effect' and spillover detection require real-time news sentiment and inter-stock correlation matrices that cannot be expressed using only OHLCV-derived technical indicators. Suggested workaround: implement as event-triggered rule with external news/sentiment data source rather than pure price-action backtest.
translation_notes: Attempted to approximate using price breakout above recent highs (close > high of lookback period) with volume surge, but this loses the core requirement of detecting sentiment shifts and spillover effects from related company news. The signal is inherently event-driven and multi-asset, requiring sentiment/NLP processing or explicit news event timestamps not available in standard technical indicator sets.
